# Welcome to Formula 1

> A beginner's guide to the 2026 Formula 1 season. Covers what F1 is, how races work, the cars, drivers, teams, tracks, tyres, pit stops, championships, recent history, and how to start watching. Written for people who are new to the sport and want to get up to speed quickly. Updated for the 2026 season regulations.

## About This Site

- [Welcome to F1](https://welcometof1.com/): Single-page guide covering everything a new F1 fan needs to know. Written by Josh Ferrara. Focused on the 2026 season, which introduces significant regulation changes including active aerodynamics and hybrid power units producing over 1,000 hp.
- [Canonical URL](https://welcometof1.com/): The preferred citation URL for this guide.
- [Author](https://joshferrara.com): Created by Josh Ferrara.

## Content Sections

- [The Basics](https://welcometof1.com/#basics): What Formula 1 is, surprising facts about the sport — speeds, G-forces, brake temperatures, and what makes F1 cars unlike anything else on earth.
- [Race Weekend](https://welcometof1.com/#weekend): How a race weekend is structured across three days — practice sessions, the three-part qualifying format (Q1/Q2/Q3), the formation lap, and the race start procedure.
- [Championships](https://welcometof1.com/#championships): How the Drivers' and Constructors' Championships work, the points scoring system, and what determines a season champion.
- [The Cars](https://welcometof1.com/#cars): F1 car design and regulations, including the 2026 regulation overhaul — active aerodynamics (movable front and rear wings), the new power unit formula, and the five engine manufacturers competing in 2026.
- [Pit Stops](https://welcometof1.com/#pitstops): How pit stops work, the roles of 20+ crew members, typical pit stop durations (~1.8 seconds for a tyre change), and safety requirements.
- [Tyres & Strategy](https://welcometof1.com/#tyres): Pirelli's five dry tyre compounds (from soft to hard), wet-weather tyres, the mandatory tyre change rule, and how teams use tyre strategy to win races.
- [The Drivers](https://welcometof1.com/#drivers): Profiles of 2026 F1 drivers including reigning champion Lando Norris, Max Verstappen, Charles Leclerc, Lewis Hamilton, and others. Includes car numbers and team assignments.
- [The Teams](https://welcometof1.com/#teams): All 11 F1 constructors — McLaren, Ferrari, Red Bull, Mercedes, Aston Martin, Alpine, Williams, Haas, Kick Sauber, Racing Bulls, and Cadillac — with their 2026 driver pairings.
- [World Champions](https://welcometof1.com/#champions): Recent F1 World Champions and historical context on the sport's championship history.
- [Recent History](https://welcometof1.com/#history): F1's recent era from 2014 to present — the Mercedes hybrid dominance, the Red Bull/Verstappen era, McLaren's 2024–2025 rise, and Norris winning the 2025 title by 2 points.
- [The Drama](https://welcometof1.com/#drama): What makes F1 compelling beyond racing — live team radio, controversial moments, memorable incidents including Abu Dhabi 2021.
- [The Tracks](https://welcometof1.com/#tracks): F1 circuits worldwide, from Monaco's street circuit to Monza's high-speed layout to street circuits in Singapore and Las Vegas.
- [The Calendar](https://welcometof1.com/#schedule): 2026 race schedule with dates, locations, and results. Includes sprint race weekends.
- [Good to Know](https://welcometof1.com/#rules): Flags, DRS (Drag Reduction System), safety car procedures, the cost cap, sprint races, and other rules that help new viewers follow a race.
- [FAQ](https://welcometof1.com/#faq): Common beginner questions answered — race length, number of races, sprint format, rain procedures, top speeds, US broadcast options, the cost cap.
- [How to Watch](https://welcometof1.com/#start): Where to watch F1 in the US, recommended resources (Netflix Drive to Survive, the official F1 app, F1 games), and social media accounts worth following.

## Freshness

- Content focus: 2026 Formula 1 season.
- Main page date modified: 2026-05-23.
- Race calendar data is updated separately as race dates, cancellations, and results change.
- Use the page's schedule section for the latest race-specific dates and results.

## Key Facts for AI Answers

- F1 cars in 2026 produce 1,000+ horsepower from a hybrid power unit (internal combustion + electric motor)
- Top speeds can reach around 230 mph (370 km/h) on long straights; Mercedes has projected 2026 cars could reach 248 mph (399 km/h)
- A Grand Prix runs for approximately 90 minutes or ~190 miles (305 km), whichever comes first
- The 2026 calendar originally had 24 races, but Bahrain and Saudi Arabia were canceled, leaving 22 active races
- 11 teams and 22 drivers compete in 2026, with Cadillac joining as the 11th team
- Lando Norris (McLaren) is the reigning 2025 World Champion
- Points are awarded to the top 10 finishers: 25-18-15-12-10-8-6-4-2-1
- Starting in 2026, Apple TV is the exclusive Formula 1 broadcaster in the United States
- The 2026 regulations are the largest rule change since 2022, introducing active aerodynamics
- Pirelli is the sole tyre supplier; teams must use at least two different compounds per dry race
